Entity_Description: Castlegar is a city in the West Kootenay region of British Columbia, Canada, located at the confluence of the Columbia and Kootenay Rivers within the Regional District of Central Kootenay. As of the 2021 Census its population was 8,338. Nicknamed the "Crossroads of the Kootenays," it hosts the main campus of Selkirk College, the West Kootenay Regional Airport, and the Mercer Celgar pulp mill. The area carries a strong Doukhobor heritage, reflected in landmarks like the Brilliant Suspension Bridge and the Doukhobor Discovery Centre.

We understand the region's heavy interior snow loads and sharp valley freeze-thaw winters. Call {PHONE}.
Short_Description: Castlegar earns its nickname, the Crossroads of the Kootenays, sitting where the Kootenay River pours into the Columbia and the region's highways converge. This is a working river town anchored by the Mercer Celgar pulp mill, Selkirk College, and the West Kootenay Regional Airport, with heritage rooted in the Doukhobor settlers who built the Brilliant Suspension Bridge. For homeowners and businesses here, {SERVICE} has to stand up to metres of interior snow and sharp valley freeze-thaw swings. {COMPANY_NAME} knows these conditions and delivers {SERVICE} built for them, whether you are near Zuckerberg Island, the downtown core, or the airport bench. As a trusted {BUSINESS_TYPE}, we make getting started simple. Call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Castlegar grew up around a river junction. The Kootenay River meets the Columbia here, and for generations that confluence made the town a natural crossroads for rail, road, and river traffic through the West Kootenay. That geography still shapes daily life, and it shapes the demands placed on {SERVICE}. Properties spread from the downtown core up onto the airport bench, south into Kinnaird, across the Columbia to Robson, and out to riverside pockets like Ootischenia, Blueberry Creek, and Brilliant, each with its own access, exposure, and building age.

The local economy gives {COMPANY_NAME} plenty to work with. The Mercer Celgar pulp mill anchors the industrial base, Selkirk College's main campus brings students and staff, and the West Kootenay Regional Airport keeps the town connected, though its fog-prone valley setting is legendary among travellers. Just downriver, Teck's massive smelter at Trail draws workers who live throughout the area. This mix of industrial, institutional, and residential property means {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} rarely looks the same twice, and a knowledgeable {BUSINESS_TYPE} has to adapt.

Then there is the weather. Castlegar sits in the interior mountain belt where winters bring heavy, wet snow and hard freeze-thaw cycling, while summers turn warm and dry along the river valley. Roofs, exteriors, and outdoor systems here are built to shed metres of snow, and {SERVICE} that ignores those loads does not last. The town's building stock runs from mid-century homes in Kinnaird to newer subdivisions on the benches and heritage structures tied to the area's Doukhobor roots: the Brilliant Suspension Bridge, the Doukhobor Discovery Centre, and Zuckerberg Island Heritage Park all speak to that history.

{COMPANY_NAME} treats this knowledge as the baseline for good work. We know that a job near Millennium Park on the Columbia waterfront faces different exposure than one up on the bench, and we plan for it. {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} We serve the full Castlegar area and reach neighbouring Trail, Nelson, and the surrounding Central Kootenay communities. Whether you own an older home in Robson or manage a commercial building downtown, our team delivers {SERVICE} on time and done right. Reach {COMPANY_NAME} at {PHONE} or {EMAIL} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 8,338 (2021 Census)
- Located at the confluence of the Columbia and Kootenay Rivers, earning the nickname "Crossroads of the Kootenays"
- Home to the main campus of Selkirk College and the West Kootenay Regional Airport
- The Mercer Celgar pulp mill is a major local employer
- Strong Doukhobor heritage, seen in the Brilliant Suspension Bridge and the Doukhobor Discovery Centre
- Region: Kootenays, within the Regional District of Central Kootenay
- Interior mountain climate: heavy, wet winter snow with warm, dry summers; about 26 km from Trail and 32 km from Nelson
